target.title: feat(hooks): add agent:turn:end and agent:turn:saved internal hook events
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: ACP turn completion updates latency/error stats, but there was no internal hook surface that distinguished "the runtime turn ended" from "the completed turn is saved and safe for post-turn readers."
- Why it matters: Existing signals are per-message, sub-turn, or session-level. Post-turn automation such as memory, indexing, audit, and analytics needs a deterministic turn boundary, and some consumers specifically need durable session state to be readable.
- What changed: Added two narrow ACP turn internal hook events:
  - `agent:turn:end`: lifecycle boundary after ACP terminal event delivery, emitted on success and failure.
  - `agent:turn:saved`: durable-state boundary after the mirrored ACP dispatch turn has been flushed and saved.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not add `agent:turn:start`, new config, user-facing messaging changes, `CHANGELOG.md` edits, or broader lifecycle refactors.
- Review context: This keeps the #37833 lineage and implements ClawSweeper's "split timing semantics before merge" option instead of overloading one event with two timing guarantees.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #37833
- Related #60734
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Internal hook consumers can subscribe to two ACP turn lifecycle events:

| Event | Fires when | Durable session-state guarantee |
|---|---|---|
| `agent:turn:end` | The ACP runtime turn completes or fails after terminal event delivery | No |
| `agent:turn:saved` | The completed ACP dispatch turn has been flushed and saved to durable session state | Yes |

Both hooks carry `sessionKey`, `success`, `durationMs`, and optional `errorCode` on failures.

If saving the mirrored dispatch turn fails, `agent:turn:end` still fires and `agent:turn:saved` is skipped. Hook handlers are bounded fire-and-forget and are not awaited through later ACP state transitions or runtime cleanup.

No user-facing messaging or config behavior changes.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[ACP turn completes] -> [latency/error stats only]

After:
[ACP turn completes]
  -> [latency/error stats]
  -> [agent:turn:end internal hook]
  -> [save mirrored ACP dispatch turn]
  -> [agent:turn:saved internal hook]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): ACP manager with an in-process test runtime backend
- Relevant config (redacted): isolated test config/state

### Steps

1. Register internal hook listeners for `agent:turn:end` and `agent:turn:saved`.
2. Trigger a successful ACP turn through `AcpSessionManager`.
3. Trigger a failing ACP turn through `AcpSessionManager`.
4. Trigger a successful ACP turn whose save step fails.
5. Trigger ACP dispatch and verify transcript persistence runs before `agent:turn:saved`.

### Expected

- Successful saved turns emit both `agent:turn:end` and `agent:turn:saved`.
- Failed turns emit `agent:turn:end` with `success: false` and `errorCode`.
- Failed turns do not emit `agent:turn:saved` unless a future save integration explicitly succeeds for that path.
- Save failures do not suppress `agent:turn:end`; they skip `agent:turn:saved`.
- Dispatch-backed ACP turns persist transcript output before `agent:turn:saved` is scheduled.
- Hook listener failures do not break the turn lifecycle.

### Actual

- Added manager-level tests covering successful, failed, saved, skipped-save, and save-failure turns.
- Added ACP dispatch tests covering transcript persistence before `agent:turn:saved` and persistence failure surfacing before the saved event.
- Added internal-hook type guard coverage for both hook events.
- Updated hook docs to document the split timing contract.

### Local hook proof on pushed head

Command:

- `git rev-parse --short HEAD` -> `3188aa0642`
- `pnpm exec tsx pr-71399-hook-proof.ts` using a throwaway local proof script against the production `AcpSessionManager` and internal hook registry

Observed output:

```text
PR #71399 hook proof

case: success-saved
  rejectedCode: none
  order: event:text_delta -> event:done -> save:ok -> hook:agent:turn:end:success=true -> hook:agent:turn:saved:success=true
  hooks: agent:turn:end{success=true, durationMs=number, errorCode=none}, agent:turn:saved{success=true, durationMs=number, errorCode=none}

case: runtime-failure
  rejectedCode: ACP_TURN_FAILED
  order: event:error -> hook:agent:turn:end:success=false
  hooks: agent:turn:end{success=false, durationMs=number, errorCode=ACP_TURN_FAILED}

case: save-failure
  rejectedCode: none
  order: event:done -> save:throw -> hook:agent:turn:end:success=true
  hooks: agent:turn:end{success=true, durationMs=number, errorCode=none}

case: save-returned-false
  rejectedCode: none
  order: event:done -> save:false -> hook:agent:turn:end:success=true
  hooks: agent:turn:end{success=true, durationMs=number, errorCode=none}

assertions: passed
```

Reproducible test proof from the same head:

```text
✓ src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts > AcpSessionManager > emits agent:turn:end and agent:turn:saved after a successful saved ACP turn
✓ src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts > AcpSessionManager > emits agent:turn:end after terminal ACP events are delivered
✓ src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts > AcpSessionManager > emits agent:turn:end but not agent:turn:saved when the save step fails
✓ src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts > AcpSessionManager > does not emit agent:turn:saved when the save step returns false
✓ src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts > AcpSessionManager > emits agent:turn:end after a failed ACP turn
✓ src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts > tryDispatchAcpReply > persists the ACP transcript before the manager emits agent:turn:saved
✓ src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts > tryDispatchAcpReply > surfaces ACP transcript persistence failures before agent:turn:saved
✓ src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts > hooks > isAgentTurnEndEvent > ...
✓ src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts > hooks > isAgentTurnSavedEvent > ...
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 5.68s
```

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `agent:turn:end` type guard recognizes the event shape, including optional `errorCode`
  - `agent:turn:saved` type guard recognizes the event shape, including optional `errorCode`
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:end` after successful turns
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:saved` after successful saved turns
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:end` after failed turns with `errorCode: "ACP_TURN_FAILED"`
  - ACP manager skips `agent:turn:saved` when the save callback fails
  - ACP manager skips `agent:turn:saved` when the save callback returns `false`
  - terminal ACP events are delivered before `agent:turn:end`
  - ACP dispatch persists transcript output before `agent:turn:saved`
  - hook dispatch uses `fireAndForgetBoundedHook(() => triggerInternalHook(...))`
  - docs describe the separate lifecycle and durable-state semantics
- Commands run locally for this revision:
  - `pnpm test src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/automation/hooks.md src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
  - `pnpm check:test-types`
  - `git diff --check`
- What I did **not** verify:
  - a networked external `acpx` adapter process; this PR verifies the OpenClaw ACP manager, dispatch, and internal-hook seams
  - full `pnpm check:changed`; the local Testbox wrapper failed before execution because `crabbox` did not pass its own sanity check

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery

- Revert this PR to remove the new internal hook events and return ACP turn completion to stats-only behavior.
- Existing config and user-facing messaging do not need migration or rollback steps.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Hook consumers may need either runtime lifecycle completion or durable saved-state readiness, and those are different timing contracts.
  - Mitigation: Split the surface into `agent:turn:end` for lifecycle completion and `agent:turn:saved` for durable-state readiness, with docs and tests for both.
- Risk: Slow hook handlers could delay turn completion if internal hook execution were awaited.
  - Mitigation: Hook dispatch remains bounded/fire-and-forget via `fireAndForgetBoundedHook()`.

target.title: docs(sms): add Twilio A2P delivery guidance
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a short US A2P / 10DLC delivery section to the SMS channel docs.
- Clarify that OpenClaw SMS setup and US carrier/Twilio A2P approval are separate readiness gates.
- Add troubleshooting guidance for Twilio sends that are accepted by the API but later show `undelivered`, including `30034`, `30909`, and `30923`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** SMS users can complete OpenClaw webhook/pairing/outbound setup while US carrier delivery still fails because Twilio A2P 10DLC Brand, Campaign, Messaging Service, or sender-number registration is incomplete. This docs change makes that separate delivery gate visible in the SMS channel docs and points users to Twilio's source-of-truth registration/error docs.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `clawsean/sms-a2p-docs`, rebased onto upstream `origin/main` at `7e0d275f7add659d62515c382c112fcf7d84a599`. The edited page is `docs/channels/sms.md`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD -- docs/channels/sms.md
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/sms.md
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/channels/sms.md
node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the real local checkout after the docs patch:

```text
Docs MDX check passed (1 files, 136ms).
Docs formatting clean (660 files).
checked_internal_links=4544
broken_links=0
```

Also manually reviewed the current Twilio A2P 10DLC overview, registration quickstart, required business/campaign information, and Twilio error pages for `30034`, `30909`, and `30923`. Claude Foreman also reviewed the wording for coverage, flow, and legal-advice risk; verdict was "ready with small edits", and those edits were applied before opening this PR.

**Observed result after fix:** The SMS docs page parses as MDX, passes docs formatting, has no broken internal links, and now contains a concise A2P/10DLC prerequisite section plus a targeted troubleshooting note for Twilio API acceptance followed by carrier delivery failure.

**What was not tested:** I did not send live SMS for this docs-only change. I did not test a Twilio A2P campaign approval flow in the Twilio Console. I did not run full repo `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`; this PR only changes one docs page and the targeted docs checks above passed.

## AI-assisted

This PR is AI-assisted. I reviewed the generated wording, checked it against Twilio/OpenClaw docs, and applied a second-pass Claude Foreman review before opening.

target.title: [codex] Read exact X posts via FxTwitter

## Summary
- Add a key-free FxTwitter exact-post reader for public X/Twitter status URLs and numeric status IDs.
- Route exact post references through FxTwitter before falling back to xAI-backed broader `x_search`.
- Keep generic X search on the existing xAI auth path and update docs/tests/build guards for the new behavior.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** OpenClaw can read an exact public X/Twitter status URL through `x_search` without requiring an xAI API key for that exact post read.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw Gateway/Dashboard, built from this patch, restarted under LaunchAgent, listening on `127.0.0.1:18789` with the promoted runtime snapshot.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built OpenClaw, restarted the Gateway, verified `openclaw gateway status --deep`, then invoked the live Gateway HTTP tool endpoint with `curl` against `x_search` using `https://x.com/jack/status/20`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the real Gateway invocation:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "provider": "fxtwitter",
  "mode": "exact_post",
  "statusId": "20",
  "hasExpectedText": true
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live Gateway returned `provider=fxtwitter`, `mode=exact_post`, `statusId=20`, and confirmed the expected post text was present (`just setting up my twttr`). This proves the exact-post path used FxTwitter and worked on a real running OpenClaw setup.
- **What was not tested:** No known gaps for the exact public post-read path. Broader xAI-backed generic X search remains covered by targeted tests and still requires xAI auth.

## Verification
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`

target.title: fix: suppress raw provider errors in channel delivery
target.body:
Fixes #69737.

## Summary

This change blocks raw unclassified assistant `errorMessage` text at the user-facing delivery boundary.

It adds a shared `formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText(...)` wrapper around the existing assistant error formatter. Classified friendly errors still use the existing sanitizer/classification behavior, but formatter output that is only raw upstream pass-through now falls back to:

```text
LLM request failed.
```

Terminal lifecycle events and reply payload construction now use the safe wrapper for actual assistant error turns. Raw diagnostic details are not globally deleted, provider-side error objects are not changed, and internal observation fields such as `rawErrorPreview` remain available for maintainers.

The follow-up repair preserves aborted-without-error behavior by keeping non-error aborted turns on the existing formatter path, so an aborted assistant with no `errorMessage` does not create a synthetic generic error payload.

## Evidence

- Synthetic canary: `SECRET_CANARY_69737`
- Current HEAD tested: `5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f`
- Raw provider-error suppression remains scoped to `stopReason="error"`.
- Aborted-without-error payload behavior is preserved.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Terminal assistant errors with unclassified raw upstream `errorMessage` must not send that raw text through user-facing lifecycle or reply payload delivery text. The repair also preserves aborted-without-error behavior so no synthetic generic error payload is emitted for aborted assistant turns with no `errorMessage`.

target.title: feat(trajectory): allow OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES override for per-event byte cap
target.body:
## Summary

The per-event byte cap for trajectory recording (`TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES`) is
hard-coded to 256 KiB in two independent locations:

- `src/trajectory/paths.ts:8` — consumed by the core runtime writer
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.ts:35` — independent copy in the Codex app-server writer

This makes it impossible to raise the limit without rebuilding from source.
Heavy sessions (large `context.compiled` payloads, multi-tool responses) routinely
exceed 256 KiB and lose diagnostic data.

This PR:

1. Reads `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES` at startup in both writers,
   falling back to the current 256 KiB default.
2. Both core and Codex resolvers accept human-friendly suffixes (`512kb`, `2mb`, `1gb`)
or raw byte counts (`1048576`).
3. Adds unit tests in the Codex extension confirming the override and suffix paths.
4. Documents the new variable in `docs/tools/trajectory.md`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** trajectory events silently truncated at 256 KiB with no
user-configurable override.

**Environment tested:** Linux 6.8.0-106-generic (x64), OpenClaw v2026.5.7 (eeef486),
Node v24.14.1, 1212 trajectory files over 30 days.

**Steps run after the patch:**

```bash
grep -c '"truncated":true' ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.trajectory.jsonl | grep -v ':0$' | wc -l

node -e "const fs=require('fs');const d=fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8');const lines=d.split('
').filter(Boolean);const t=lines.filter(l=>{const o=JSON.parse(l);return o.data?.truncated});console.log('total:',lines.length,'truncated:',t.length)" ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/9e600bba-2bbe-4ff4-98ca-59ea5f1bb896.trajectory.jsonl
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Before — same production host as §"Environment tested", current published `openclaw` build, last 30 days of sessions:

```text
$ grep -c '"truncated":true' ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.trajectory.jsonl | grep -v ':0$' | wc -l
459
```

A single heavy session (7.3 MB transcript) had 633 truncated events out of 1891 total — 33% data loss under the default 256 KiB cap.

Earlier after-fix driver — patched branch head `76877f65f9c42b1154820dba43838ea413b3dc27`, a self-contained driver that wires the real `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` and records a synthetic oversized payload (60 keys × 5000 chars = ~293 KB after sanitization, deliberately under the inner `STRING_MAX_CHARS=32768` / `OBJECT_MAX_KEYS=64` / `DEPTH=6` limits so only the outer event-cap fires). The two phases use the **identical payload**; only the env var changes:

```text
==================================================
PHASE — default 256 KiB cap, identical payload
==================================================
>>> recorder.filePath = /root/openclaw-80681/pr80681-driver-1778935876980.trajectory.jsonl
>>> resolved env override: OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=(unset)
>>> payload: 60 keys × 5000 chars = 300000 chars total (~293 KB)
>>> recorded line bytes: 354
>>> truncated flag: true
>>> truncation originalBytes: 301095
>>> truncation limitBytes:    262144
>>> truncation reason:        trajectory-event-size-limit

==================================================
PHASE — OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1mb, identical payload
==================================================
>>> recorder.filePath = /root/openclaw-80681/pr80681-driver-1778935877004.trajectory.jsonl
>>> resolved env override: OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1mb
>>> payload: 60 keys × 5000 chars = 300000 chars total (~293 KB)
>>> recorded line bytes: 301095
>>> truncated flag: false
>>> payload key count preserved: 60
```

Current head validation — patched source checkout at PR head `f6ae71e323f5523e9ecdb894d6990d0ca5639045`, Windows NT 10.0.26100 x64, Node v24.15.0, using the real `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` path and the same oversized payload shape:

```text
PHASE default-cap
env (unset)
bytes 312
truncated true
reason trajectory-event-size-limit
field_count (truncated sentinel)

PHASE one-mb
env 1mb
bytes 301033
truncated false
reason (none)
field_count 60
```

Current head focused tests also pass after the Windows-portable test expectation fix:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/trajectory/paths.test.ts src/trajectory/runtime.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed | 2 skipped (13)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  9 passed (9)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 19.80s
```

- Phase 1 (no override): the recorded `.trajectory.jsonl` line is replaced by a 354-byte sentinel carrying `truncated:true`, `originalBytes:301095`, `limitBytes:262144`, `reason:"trajectory-event-size-limit"`. This is the current `main` behavior under the default 256 KiB cap.
- Phase 2 (`OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1mb`): same payload, same recorder. The recorded line is the full **301095 bytes** and carries `truncated:false`; all 60 fields survive at full 5000 chars each.

The byte-for-byte difference (354 → 301095, ~880×) is the direct manifestation of the override raising the per-event cap above the actual payload size. The current core-runtime fix at PR head `f6ae71e323f5523e9ecdb894d6990d0ca5639045` resolves the cap once from `params.env` at recorder creation (ClawSweeper P2), so the override is honored across the recorder lifetime rather than being re-read from ambient `process.env` per event.

The earlier focused vitest set also passed on the previous patch build:

```text
 RUN  v4.1.6

 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > returns the 256 KiB default when env is empty  125ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > returns the default when the override is whitespace only  1ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts raw byte counts  1ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts kb suffixes  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts mb suffixes  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts gb suffixes  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > falls back to the default on unparseable input  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > falls back to the default on non-positive input  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > module-load constant matches the empty-env resolver result  0ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > keeps write flags usable when O_NOFOLLOW is unavailable  9ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > records by default unless explicitly disabled  5ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > sanitizes session ids when resolving an override directory  3ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > honors explicit disablement  1ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > refuses to append through a symlinked parent directory  1ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > honors OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES override  3ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > truncates events that exceed the runtime event byte limit  4ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > accepts human-friendly byte-size suffixes for the event cap override  2ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > falls back to default on invalid suffix values  2ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
   Duration  16.93s
```

The new test case `honors OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES override` directly exercises the env-injection path at recorder creation.

**Observed result:** 459 out of 1212 trajectory files contain truncated events
(reason: `trajectory-event-size-limit`). A single heavy session
(`9e600bba`) has 633 truncated events out of 1891 total — 33% data loss.
After the patch, setting `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1048576`
eliminates truncation for sessions that fit within 1 MiB per event.
Setting `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=2mb` is equivalent.

**Not tested:** Filesystem-level I/O performance impact at very large per-event limits
(>10 MiB). The file-level cap (`TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_FILE_MAX_BYTES = 50 MiB`) still
applies and prevents unbounded disk growth.
## Addressing ClawSweeper review

### P2 — Resolve the core cap from the recorder env (`src/trajectory/runtime.ts:121`)

Fixed. `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` already captured `params.env` for `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY` enablement and trajectory path resolution; the truncation path was re-reading the per-event byte cap from ambient `process.env` on every event, which (a) silently ignored an injected `params.env` override and (b) let mid-session ambient mutations change behavior.

The truncation path now resolves the cap **once** at recorder creation from the captured `env`:

```ts
// inside createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder, after `env` is resolved
const eventMaxBytes = resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes(env);

// later, in buildEventLine
const boundedLine = truncateOversizedTrajectoryEvent(event, line, eventMaxBytes);
```

`truncateOversizedTrajectoryEvent` no longer calls `resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes()` internally; it accepts the pre-resolved `limitBytes` as a parameter. This matches the pinning semantics already used for `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY` and `resolveTrajectoryFilePath`.

### Prior review items (resolved earlier on this branch)

1. **F1 — broad SDK barrel import**: reverted to narrow subpaths.

   ```ts
   import { resolveUserPath } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime";
   import { appendRegularFile, resolveRegularFileAppendFlags } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/security-runtime";
   ```

2. **F2 — re-implemented file-write safety**: restored shared helpers. The hot append path uses `appendRegularFile()` and `resolveRegularFileAppendFlags()` from `security-runtime` (no local symlink/hardlink/nlink rewrite for the append path).

3. **F3 — invalid-fallback test using a valid value**: now uses a truly malformed value.

   ```ts
   env: { OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES: "abc-not-a-size" },
   ```

4. **F4 — CHANGELOG.md conflict**: rebased on latest `main`.

### Note on `lstatSync` calls in `writeTrajectoryPointerBestEffort`

Two `lstatSync(...).isSymbolicLink()` checks remain in `writeTrajectoryPointerBestEffort`. They are **not** related to the F2 finding:

- They guard the **trajectory pointer file** (`*.trajectory-path.json`), a separate sidecar JSON file written via `openSync` + `writeFileSync` to an attacker-untrusted path.
- The trajectory main file (`*.trajectory.jsonl`) append path uses `appendRegularFile()` and is unaffected.
- These pointer-specific symlink checks were added on this PR (no equivalent in current `main`) to harden the pointer write path. They are independent of the per-event byte cap feature, but are kept because removing them would lose the symlink protection on the pointer file.

If reviewers prefer the pointer-write symlink hardening to land in a follow-up PR rather than this one, happy to move them out — please advise.

target.title: fix(plugins): block untrusted workspace setup-only channel loads
target.body:
## Summary - Problem: latest `main` already keeps execution-facing channel setup discovery on the trusted catalog path, but lower-level setup-only scoped plugin loads could still import and execute disabled workspace channel plugins when callers passed explicit `onlyPluginIds`. - What changed: this PR restores the lower-level security boundary from #64154 by blocking disabled workspace channel plugins before setup-only import, adds a registry backstop that rejects channel registration from disabled workspace plugins, and ports the complementary #85774 guard so a broken trusted setup-entry channel reports a diagnostic instead of aborting sibling plugin loading. - What latest `main` already kept from #64154: `channels add`, onboarding/setup resolution, and setup discovery now use trust-aware catalog helpers; untrusted workspace shadows fall back to bundled/non-workspace entries; workspace-only candidates remain visible only in the setup wizard discovery bucket; the raw catalog listing is kept behind an internal/deprecated helper. - Remaining work before merge: no known #64154 hardening gap remains in code after this PR. The remaining gates are CI plus any maintainer-requested live external-plugin proof. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Replaces/supersedes #64154 - Includes the valuable complementary setup-entry error-isolation fix from #85774 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Current Main vs This PR The #64154 design had two layers: 1. Catalog callers should resolve channels through trusted helpers so setup/add flows do not select untrusted workspace shadows. 2. The loader/registry should enforce the same trust boundary before any plugin module can execute, so lower-level callers cannot bypass the catalog layer. Latest `main` still preserves the first layer. `channels add`, setup/onboarding, and channel setup plugin resolution use `listTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntries` / `getTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntry`; the setup wizard's installable display can still show workspace-only candidates through the lenient discovery helper without treating them as executable/installed. This PR fills the second layer. It prevents explicit setup-only scoped loads from importing disabled workspace channel plugins, rejects disabled workspace channel registrations at the registry boundary, and keeps trusted workspace overrides working. It also ports #85774's try/catch around `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)`, which addresses a related but distinct resilience gap: trusted setup-entry plugins are allowed to load, but one malformed registration should not stop healthy sibling plugins from loading. ## Diagram ```mermaid graph TD SOURCES["Plugin source directories"] --> MANIFEST["Manifest registry
(pluginId + origin + channels)"] MANIFEST -->|"setup / onboard / channels add"| TRUSTED["Trusted catalog helpers
strict execution list
untrusted workspace shadows fall back"] MANIFEST -->|"setup wizard display"| DISCOVERY["Setup discovery helper
lenient display list
workspace-only candidates visible"] MANIFEST -->|"gateway or explicit scoped setup load"| LOADER["loadOpenClawPlugins()"] TRUSTED --> SETUP["setup/add snapshot request"] DISCOVERY --> UI["installable UI bucket only
not an execution decision"] SETUP --> LOADER LOADER --> GATE{"This PR:
workspace channel plugin enabled?"} GATE -->|"disabled workspace"| BLOCK["reject before setup-only import
no plugin code executes"] GATE -->|"trusted workspace or non-workspace"| IMPORT["import setup/runtime entry"] IMPORT --> REGISTER{"This PR + #85774:
registerChannel succeeds?"} REGISTER -->|"yes"| REGISTRY["snapshot/runtime registry"] REGISTER -->|"throws"| DIAG["record plugin diagnostic
continue loading siblings"] REGISTRY --> SNAPUSE["validateInput / applyAccountConfig
write config / lifecycle callbacks"] REGISTRY --> RUNTIME["gateway/runtime channel lookup"] BLOCK:::fixed GATE:::fixed DIAG:::fixed TRUSTED:::kept DISCOVERY:::display UI:::display classDef fixed fill:#d4edda,stroke:#28a745,stroke-width:2px classDef kept fill:#d9edf7,stroke:#31708f,stroke-width:2px classDef display fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107,stroke-width:2px ``` The important boundary is now shared: catalog helpers decide which channel catalog entries are trusted for setup/add flows, and the loader/registry enforce the same rule before runtime code can execute. If a trusted setup-entry channel still fails during registration, the error is isolated to that plugin and sibling plugins continue loading. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: untrusted workspace channel plugins should not execute during setup-only scoped loads; disabled workspace channel registrations should be rejected at registry assembly; trusted setup-entry channel registration failures should not abort sibling plugin loading. - Real environment tested: local Linux repo checkout rebased on `upstream/main` `717003aaffec3d41792424079a52beaf2bd92a5c`; PR head `55811ad9edce27d49decb99c7564fba5c4138fb3`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: - `pnpm docs:list` - `pnpm test src/commands/onboard-channels.e2e.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/channel-plugin-resolution.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/discovery.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/workspace-shadow-bypass.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/channels.add.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm build` - `pnpm check:changed` - Evidence after fix: `src/commands/onboard-channels.e2e.test.ts` passes 16/16 after exposing `listRawChannelPluginCatalogEntries` through the same mock/actual fallback as the old catalog helper; the focused loader/registry/catalog/channel setup tests pass after the rebase; `pnpm build` and the final `pnpm check:changed` pass; `CHANGELOG.md` and stale transcripts tool-display metadata are no longer in the final branch diff. - Observed result after fix: disabled workspace shadows no longer execute through scoped setup-only loads; trusted workspace overrides remain available; disabled workspace channel registrations are rejected as a registry backstop; malformed trusted setup-entry channel registration records a diagnostic and sibling plugins keep loading. - What was not tested: live channel setup against a real external workspace plugin outside the loader test harness. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the same minimal setup-only repro failed on latest `origin/main` before this branch because the marker file was created during scoped setup-only load. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: the scoped setup-only loader path treated explicit `onlyPluginIds` as sufficient to load a disabled workspace channel plugin, so workspace trust was bypassed below the trusted-catalog call sites. - Missing detection / guardrail: registry assembly still accepted channel registration from disabled workspace plugins if a lower-level caller imported one. - Complementary #85774 root cause: the setup-entry loader loop caught surrounding registration failures, but `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)` itself was unguarded, so one throwing trusted channel registration could abort sibling plugin loading. - Contributing context: latest `main` already absorbed the higher-level `channels add` / trusted-catalog call-site fixes from #64154, but not this lower-level loader/registry backstop or the setup-entry error-isolation guard. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `src/plugins/loader.test.ts`, `src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts`, `src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts`, and existing channel setup/add tests. - Scenario the test should lock in: explicit scoped setup-only loads must reject disabled workspace channel plugins but keep trusted workspace channel plugins available; registry assembly must reject disabled workspace channel registrations; trusted setup-entry registration errors must be recorded without aborting healthy sibling plugins. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bugs live at loader/registry/catalog boundaries and require real plugin import behavior plus registration-mode assertions. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): `only setup-loads a disabled channel plugin when the caller scopes to the selected plugin` still covers the non-workspace path. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Untrusted workspace channel shadows no longer execute during setup-only scoped loads. - Trusted workspace channel plugins remain available to setup-time scoped loading. - A broken trusted setup-entry channel registration now surfaces as a plugin diagnostic instead of stopping sibling plugin loading. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux - Runtime/container: Node 22 local repo checkout - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): plugin loader / channel plugin registry / channel setup catalog - Relevant config (redacted): workspace plugins enabled; untrusted plugin omitted from allowlist ### Steps 1. Create a workspace channel plugin fixture with `channels` metadata and a side-effect marker write in `register()`. 2. Load it through `loadOpenClawPlugins({ includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true, forceSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true, onlyPluginIds: [...] })`. 3. Compare disabled workspace behavior against trusted workspace behavior. 4. Load a setup-entry channel whose channel config getter throws during `registerChannel` alongside a healthy sibling plugin. ### Expected - Disabled workspace channel plugin stays disabled and does not execute. - Trusted workspace channel plugin remains available to setup-only loading. - A throwing trusted setup-entry channel records a diagnostic and does not prevent sibling plugins from loading. ### Actual - This branch matches the expected behavior. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets in test output - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: untrusted/trusted workspace scoped setup-only regressions, disabled workspace registry guard, trusted-catalog filtering semantics, onboarding channel setup e2e catalog mock compatibility, `channels add` workspace shadow fallback, #85774 setup-entry `registerChannel` error isolation, `pnpm build`, and final `pnpm check:changed`. - Edge cases checked: trusted workspace override still loads in setup-only mode; non-workspace disabled plugin behavior stays unchanged; setup wizard discovery still keeps workspace-only install candidates visible without treating them as installed/executable. - What you did **not** verify: a live end-to-end channel setup outside the loader test harness. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) No. This is an intentional security hardening break for disabled workspace channel plugins that were previously reachable through setup-only scoped loads. - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No automatic migration. Operators who intentionally develop or run a workspace/linked channel plugin must explicitly enable/trust that plugin before setup-time code can run. - If yes, exact upgrade steps: use the normal plugin enable/trust flow for the workspace plugin before running channel setup. The behavior is documented in `docs/cli/plugins.md`. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: a trusted workspace plugin could accidentally get blocked if the trust check were too broad. - Mitigation: regression coverage explicitly keeps the trusted workspace scoped-load path green. - Risk: setup discovery could accidentally hide workspace-only install candidates. - Mitigation: trusted-catalog and discovery tests keep the strict execution list separate from the lenient setup wizard display list. - Risk: an existing disabled workspace channel plugin that relied on setup-only execution now fails closed. - Mitigation: this is the intended security behavior; the docs now state that workspace-origin plugins are not imported or executed until explicitly enabled/trusted. ### Proof 1: Pre-Fix Reproduction on origin/main - Behavior addressed: Untrusted workspace channel plugin (evi1-channel-probe) executes its register() function via the setup-only scoped load path when includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true is passed to loadOpenClawPlugins, bypassing the trust boundary. - Real environment tested: Crabbox docker, provider=docker, container=391883b7652bcb464c523f64ddcabb9bcad47f92e434737b7cc5391f7bc0922f, run=cbx_6dadb5d505eb, checkout SHA=ead847f6065ac36031f9e7a95dd1ee90f171f79c (origin/main) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) `git fetch --depth=1 origin main && git checkout FETCH_HEAD` inside Crabbox container; (2) `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`; (3) Deploy evi1-channel-probe plugin to /tmp/proof1-workspace/.openclaw/extensions/evi1-channel-probe/; (4) Confirm /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence absent; (5) Run `node probe-untrusted.mjs` with onlyPluginIds=["evi1-channel-probe"], includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins=true, activate=false, cache=false; (6) Check /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence existence. - Evidence after fix: Marker file /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence CREATED with content "evi1-probe-executed\n". Plugin status="disabled" with channelSetups=[{id:"evi1-channel-probe"}] — the setup-only path imported and executed the untrusted workspace plugin code. - Observed result after fix: On origin/main (pre-fix), the untrusted workspace channel plugin code ran despite being disabled. The plugin appeared in channelSetups (not channels), status was "disabled", yet register() executed and created the marker file. This confirms the bypass vulnerability. - What was not tested: CLI blackbox path, real channels add flow ### Proof 2: Post-Fix Verification on PR Head - Behavior addressed: The PR fix blocks untrusted workspace channel plugins from executing during setup-only scoped loads; disabled workspace channel registrations are rejected at registry assembly; trusted workspace plugins with plugins.allow still work; broken trusted setup-entry registerChannel records a diagnostic without aborting sibling plugins. - Real environment tested: Crabbox docker, provider=docker, container=df29879e2faef7ac8794cba519c1988aff410316a3718d90c6f638cd9977ce15, lease=cbx_0f687f5da081, checkout SHA=b05a7b14fe9bd6c119b3b7fe60a1967d0987804c (PR head at time of test; refreshed #86953 head is `2a9a2d79fd498be5fc62e83634a544f873b67b4c`) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) `git fetch origin pull/82579/head:pr-head && git checkout pr-head` inside Crabbox container; (2) `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`; (3) `grep -r "channel registration rejected for disabled workspace plugin" dist/` — fix code found in dist/loader-DM-dphfh.js; (4) Deploy evi1-channel-probe (untrusted), confirm marker ABSENT after probe; (5) Deploy evi1-trusted-probe (with plugins.allow), confirm marker EXISTS; (6) Deploy evi1-broken-register + evi1-sibling-probe (with plugins.allow), confirm sibling marker EXISTS and diagnostic recorded; (7) Run focused tests: `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts` (142 pass), `pnpm test src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts` (2 pass), `pnpm test src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts` (5 pass), `pnpm test src/commands/channel-setup/workspace-shadow-bypass.test.ts` (6 pass); (8) `pnpm check:changed` — 0 errors. - Evidence after fix: Untrusted probe: MARKER_ABSENT, exit code 0 (clean block, not crash). Plugin status="disabled", channelSetups=[]. Trusted probe: MARKER_EXISTS, content "evi1-probe-executed\n". Plugin status="loaded", channels and channelSetups populated. Error isolation: broken-register records diagnostic (level=error, pluginId=evi1-broken-register), process exit code 0. Sibling marker EXISTS, content "evi1-sibling-probe-executed\n". - Observed result after fix: The fix blocks untrusted workspace channel plugin execution. Untrusted plugin stays disabled with empty channelSetups. Trusted workspace plugin with plugins.allow still loads and registers channels. Broken trusted registerChannel records a diagnostic and sibling plugins continue loading. All focused tests pass. - What was not tested: CLI blackbox path, real channels add flow, 3 pre-existing test failures in channel-auth.test.ts (2) and channels.remove.test.ts (1) unrelated to this PR Crabbox cross-reference: Proof 1 provider=docker, container=391883b7652bcb464c523f64ddcabb9bcad47f92e434737b7cc5391f7bc0922f, run=cbx_6dadb5d505eb, SHA=ead847f6065ac36031f9e7a95dd1ee90f171f79c (origin/main). target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(plugins): block untrusted workspace setup-only channel loads
target.body:
## Summary - Problem: latest `main` already keeps execution-facing channel setup discovery on the trusted catalog path, but lower-level setup-only scoped plugin loads could still import and execute disabled workspace channel plugins when callers passed explicit `onlyPluginIds`. - What changed: this PR restores the lower-level security boundary from #64154 by blocking disabled workspace channel plugins before setup-only import, adds a registry backstop that rejects channel registration from disabled workspace plugins, and ports the complementary #85774 guard so a broken trusted setup-entry channel reports a diagnostic instead of aborting sibling plugin loading. - What latest `main` already kept from #64154: `channels add`, onboarding/setup resolution, and setup discovery now use trust-aware catalog helpers; untrusted workspace shadows fall back to bundled/non-workspace entries; workspace-only candidates remain visible only in the setup wizard discovery bucket; the raw catalog listing is kept behind an internal/deprecated helper. - Remaining work before merge: no known #64154 hardening gap remains in code after this PR. The remaining gates are CI plus any maintainer-requested live external-plugin proof. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Replaces/supersedes #64154 - Includes the valuable complementary setup-entry error-isolation fix from #85774 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Current Main vs This PR The #64154 design had two layers: 1. Catalog callers should resolve channels through trusted helpers so setup/add flows do not select untrusted workspace shadows. 2. The loader/registry should enforce the same trust boundary before any plugin module can execute, so lower-level callers cannot bypass the catalog layer. Latest `main` still preserves the first layer. `channels add`, setup/onboarding, and channel setup plugin resolution use `listTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntries` / `getTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntry`; the setup wizard's installable display can still show workspace-only candidates through the lenient discovery helper without treating them as executable/installed. This PR fills the second layer. It prevents explicit setup-only scoped loads from importing disabled workspace channel plugins, rejects disabled workspace channel registrations at the registry boundary, and keeps trusted workspace overrides working. It also ports #85774's try/catch around `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)`, which addresses a related but distinct resilience gap: trusted setup-entry plugins are allowed to load, but one malformed registration should not stop healthy sibling plugins from loading. ## Diagram ```mermaid graph TD SOURCES["Plugin source directories"] --> MANIFEST["Manifest registry
(pluginId + origin + channels)"] MANIFEST -->|"setup / onboard / channels add"| TRUSTED["Trusted catalog helpers
strict execution list
untrusted workspace shadows fall back"] MANIFEST -->|"setup wizard display"| DISCOVERY["Setup discovery helper
lenient display list
workspace-only candidates visible"] MANIFEST -->|"gateway or explicit scoped setup load"| LOADER["loadOpenClawPlugins()"] TRUSTED --> SETUP["setup/add snapshot request"] DISCOVERY --> UI["installable UI bucket only
not an execution decision"] SETUP --> LOADER LOADER --> GATE{"This PR:
workspace channel plugin enabled?"} GATE -->|"disabled workspace"| BLOCK["reject before setup-only import
no plugin code executes"] GATE -->|"trusted workspace or non-workspace"| IMPORT["import setup/runtime entry"] IMPORT --> REGISTER{"This PR + #85774:
registerChannel succeeds?"} REGISTER -->|"yes"| REGISTRY["snapshot/runtime registry"] REGISTER -->|"throws"| DIAG["record plugin diagnostic
continue loading siblings"] REGISTRY --> SNAPUSE["validateInput / applyAccountConfig
write config / lifecycle callbacks"] REGISTRY --> RUNTIME["gateway/runtime channel lookup"] BLOCK:::fixed GATE:::fixed DIAG:::fixed TRUSTED:::kept DISCOVERY:::display UI:::display classDef fixed fill:#d4edda,stroke:#28a745,stroke-width:2px classDef kept fill:#d9edf7,stroke:#31708f,stroke-width:2px classDef display fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107,stroke-width:2px ``` The important boundary is now shared: catalog helpers decide which channel catalog entries are trusted for setup/add flows, and the loader/registry enforce the same rule before runtime code can execute. If a trusted setup-entry channel still fails during registration, the error is isolated to that plugin and sibling plugins continue loading. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: untrusted workspace channel plugins should not execute during setup-only scoped loads; disabled workspace channel registrations should be rejected at registry assembly; trusted setup-entry channel registration failures should not abort sibling plugin loading. - Real environment tested: local Linux repo checkout rebased on `upstream/main` `717003aaffec3d41792424079a52beaf2bd92a5c`; PR head `55811ad9edce27d49decb99c7564fba5c4138fb3`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: - `pnpm docs:list` - `pnpm test src/commands/onboard-channels.e2e.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/channel-plugin-resolution.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/discovery.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/workspace-shadow-bypass.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/channels.add.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm build` - `pnpm check:changed` - Evidence after fix: `src/commands/onboard-channels.e2e.test.ts` passes 16/16 after exposing `listRawChannelPluginCatalogEntries` through the same mock/actual fallback as the old catalog helper; the focused loader/registry/catalog/channel setup tests pass after the rebase; `pnpm build` and the final `pnpm check:changed` pass; `CHANGELOG.md` and stale transcripts tool-display metadata are no longer in the final branch diff. - Observed result after fix: disabled workspace shadows no longer execute through scoped setup-only loads; trusted workspace overrides remain available; disabled workspace channel registrations are rejected as a registry backstop; malformed trusted setup-entry channel registration records a diagnostic and sibling plugins keep loading. - What was not tested: live channel setup against a real external workspace plugin outside the loader test harness. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the same minimal setup-only repro failed on latest `origin/main` before this branch because the marker file was created during scoped setup-only load. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: the scoped setup-only loader path treated explicit `onlyPluginIds` as sufficient to load a disabled workspace channel plugin, so workspace trust was bypassed below the trusted-catalog call sites. - Missing detection / guardrail: registry assembly still accepted channel registration from disabled workspace plugins if a lower-level caller imported one. - Complementary #85774 root cause: the setup-entry loader loop caught surrounding registration failures, but `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)` itself was unguarded, so one throwing trusted channel registration could abort sibling plugin loading. - Contributing context: latest `main` already absorbed the higher-level `channels add` / trusted-catalog call-site fixes from #64154, but not this lower-level loader/registry backstop or the setup-entry error-isolation guard. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `src/plugins/loader.test.ts`, `src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts`, `src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts`, and existing channel setup/add tests. - Scenario the test should lock in: explicit scoped setup-only loads must reject disabled workspace channel plugins but keep trusted workspace channel plugins available; registry assembly must reject disabled workspace channel registrations; trusted setup-entry registration errors must be recorded without aborting healthy sibling plugins. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bugs live at loader/registry/catalog boundaries and require real plugin import behavior plus registration-mode assertions. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): `only setup-loads a disabled channel plugin when the caller scopes to the selected plugin` still covers the non-workspace path. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Untrusted workspace channel shadows no longer execute during setup-only scoped loads. - Trusted workspace channel plugins remain available to setup-time scoped loading. - A broken trusted setup-entry channel registration now surfaces as a plugin diagnostic instead of stopping sibling plugin loading. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux - Runtime/container: Node 22 local repo checkout - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): plugin loader / channel plugin registry / channel setup catalog - Relevant config (redacted): workspace plugins enabled; untrusted plugin omitted from allowlist ### Steps 1. Create a workspace channel plugin fixture with `channels` metadata and a side-effect marker write in `register()`. 2. Load it through `loadOpenClawPlugins({ includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true, forceSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true, onlyPluginIds: [...] })`. 3. Compare disabled workspace behavior against trusted workspace behavior. 4. Load a setup-entry channel whose channel config getter throws during `registerChannel` alongside a healthy sibling plugin. ### Expected - Disabled workspace channel plugin stays disabled and does not execute. - Trusted workspace channel plugin remains available to setup-only loading. - A throwing trusted setup-entry channel records a diagnostic and does not prevent sibling plugins from loading. ### Actual - This branch matches the expected behavior. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets in test output - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: untrusted/trusted workspace scoped setup-only regressions, disabled workspace registry guard, trusted-catalog filtering semantics, onboarding channel setup e2e catalog mock compatibility, `channels add` workspace shadow fallback, #85774 setup-entry `registerChannel` error isolation, `pnpm build`, and final `pnpm check:changed`. - Edge cases checked: trusted workspace override still loads in setup-only mode; non-workspace disabled plugin behavior stays unchanged; setup wizard discovery still keeps workspace-only install candidates visible without treating them as installed/executable. - What you did **not** verify: a live end-to-end channel setup outside the loader test harness. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) No. This is an intentional security hardening break for disabled workspace channel plugins that were previously reachable through setup-only scoped loads. - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No automatic migration. Operators who intentionally develop or run a workspace/linked channel plugin must explicitly enable/trust that plugin before setup-time code can run. - If yes, exact upgrade steps: use the normal plugin enable/trust flow for the workspace plugin before running channel setup. The behavior is documented in `docs/cli/plugins.md`. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: a trusted workspace plugin could accidentally get blocked if the trust check were too broad. - Mitigation: regression coverage explicitly keeps the trusted workspace scoped-load path green. - Risk: setup discovery could accidentally hide workspace-only install candidates. - Mitigation: trusted-catalog and discovery tests keep the strict execution list separate from the lenient setup wizard display list. - Risk: an existing disabled workspace channel plugin that relied on setup-only execution now fails closed. - Mitigation: this is the intended security behavior; the docs now state that workspace-origin plugins are not imported or executed until explicitly enabled/trusted. ### Proof 1: Pre-Fix Reproduction on origin/main - Behavior addressed: Untrusted workspace channel plugin (evi1-channel-probe) executes its register() function via the setup-only scoped load path when includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true is passed to loadOpenClawPlugins, bypassing the trust boundary. - Real environment tested: Crabbox docker, provider=docker, container=391883b7652bcb464c523f64ddcabb9bcad47f92e434737b7cc5391f7bc0922f, run=cbx_6dadb5d505eb, checkout SHA=ead847f6065ac36031f9e7a95dd1ee90f171f79c (origin/main) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) `git fetch --depth=1 origin main && git checkout FETCH_HEAD` inside Crabbox container; (2) `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`; (3) Deploy evi1-channel-probe plugin to /tmp/proof1-workspace/.openclaw/extensions/evi1-channel-probe/; (4) Confirm /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence absent; (5) Run `node probe-untrusted.mjs` with onlyPluginIds=["evi1-channel-probe"], includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins=true, activate=false, cache=false; (6) Check /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence existence. - Evidence after fix: Marker file /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence CREATED with content "evi1-probe-executed\n". This confirms the bypass vulnerability. - What was not tested: CLI blackbox path, real channels add flow ### Proof 2: Post-Fix Verification on PR Head - Behavior addressed: The PR fix blocks untrusted workspace channel plugins from executing during setup-only scoped loads; disabled workspace channel registrations are rejected at registry assembly; trusted workspace plugins with plugins.allow still work; broken trusted setup-entry registerChannel records a diagnostic without aborting sibling plugins. - Real environment tested: Crabbox docker, provider=docker, container=df29879e2faef7ac8794cba519c1988aff410316a3718d90c6f638cd9977ce15, lease=cbx_0f687f5da081, checkout SHA=b05a7b14fe9bd6c119b3b7fe60a1967d0987804c (PR head at time of test; refreshed #86953 head is `2a9a2d79fd498be5fc62e83634a544f873b67b4c`) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) `git fetch origin pull/82579/head:pr-head && git checkout pr-head` inside Crabbox container; (2) `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`; (3) `grep -r "channel registration rejected for disabled workspace plugin" dist/` — fix code found in dist/loader-DM-dphfh.js; (4) Deploy evi1-channel-probe (untrusted), confirm marker ABSENT after probe; (5) Deploy evi1-trusted-probe (with plugins.allow), confirm marker EXISTS; (6) Deploy evi1-broken-register + evi1-sibling-probe (with plugins.allow), confirm sibling marker EXISTS and diagnostic recorded; (7) Run focused tests: `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts` (142 pass), `pnpm test src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts` (2 pass), `pnpm test src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts` (5 pass), `pnpm test src/commands/channel-setup/workspace-shadow-bypass.test.ts` (6 pass); (8) `pnpm check:changed` — 0 errors. - Evidence after fix: Untrusted probe: MARKER_ABSENT, exit code 0 (clean block, not crash). target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
